page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the ARMv9 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with 1.0 thread per core, backed by 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The hardware configuration includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, managed under the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8gd.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths of 296.81 GB/sec and 124.13 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also rank in the strong tier. However, while text processing and database operations show strong performance, LLM inference tasks on medium 7B models reveal a performance tradeoff, with prompt processing speeds falling into the weak tier at 31.26 tokens/sec.

This instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from local high-speed NVMe storage. The dedicated ARMv9 cores and high memory bandwidth make it highly suitable for applications such as database operations, data compression, and multi-threaded processing, whereas large-scale LLM inference is constrained by the lack of hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of memory, 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and an 8 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance with a strong uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 296.81 GB/sec, alongside strong database operations and multi-threaded decompression capabilities. However, it exhibits a performance tradeoff in LLM inference, where prompt processing for medium models is relatively weak. This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, database hosting, and data compression tasks requiring high-speed local storage.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 physical cores
32.0 GB of system memory and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-threaded decompression performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database operations, data compression, and compute-heavy workloads
